summary: Undergraduate Computer Science student at Sri Venkateswara College of Engineering with a CGPA of 8.68 and hands-on experience building distributed systems, AI pipelines, and full-stack applications. Proficient in Java, Python, and JavaScript, with practical exposure to frameworks and tools such as LangChain, LangGraph, FastAPI, and cloud infrastructure through Internship and project work.

bullets: Developed modular microservices using FastAPI and React, improving system scalability and reducing deployment cycles through strict adherence to SOLID principles.
Built LangGraph-based RAG workflows for retrieval, validation, and response generation, improving response consistency for internal knowledge-management tasks.
Optimized PostgreSQL query performance through execution-plan analysis and targeted indexing, reducing average API response latency from 250ms to 160ms under concurrent workloads.
Implemented automated unit and integration tests using PyTest, increasing code coverage to 85% and improving regression detection during feature releases.
Built a real-time IoT monitoring system integrating 10+ ESP32 sensors with a React dashboard for telemetry visualization.
Designed an MQTT communication pipeline that reduced data transmission overhead compared to HTTP, ensuring sub-500ms end-to-end latency.
Engineered a WebSocket-based bridge to stream real-time event data to 50+ concurrent clients and automated reconnection.

projects.name: OmniKnow: Enterprise AI Knowledge Graph & Retrieval System
projects.description: Developed a Enterprise Knowledge Management system at Genworx AI that utilizes a hybrid search approach (Semantic + Keyword) to allow employees to query thousands of internal technical documents with high precision.
bullets: Architected a Hybrid Search engine combining pgvector for semantic retrieval and BM25 for keyword matching, increasing document retrieval precision for technical jargon.
Implemented temporal-aware retrieval in the RAG pipeline by prioritizing document recency alongside semantic similarity, improving relevance for time-sensitive internal knowledge queries.
Implemented a multi-stage re-ranking pipeline using a Cross-Encoder model, which improved the Top-3 answer accuracy (Hit Rate).
Deployed the system using Docker and GCP (Google Cloud Platform), supporting 500+ concurrent internal users during the beta phase.

projects.name: LexiAI: Multi-Agent Legal Research Engine
projects.description: Architected a Multi-Agent RAG System using LangGraph, orchestrating a specialized Generator Agent (LLM) and a Discriminator Agent to validate responses, significantly reducing hallucination rates and enabling web-based precedent matching for enhanced legal research.
bullets: Architected a Multi-Agent RAG System using LangGraph, orchestrating a Generator Agent and a Discriminator Agent to validate responses, reducing hallucination rates and enabling web-based precedent matching.
Implemented Semantic Search using vector embeddings (SentenceTransformers) over a corpus of 200+ legal documents, enabling context-aware retrieval beyond keyword matching.
Designed an asynchronous ingestion pipeline using asyncio to parse and embed PDF documents concurrently, optimizing throughput for large datasets.
Built a RESTful API layer with exponential retry logic handling multiple concurrent web requests.

projects.name: High-Concurrency Movie Booking System
projects.description: Engineered a high-concurrency ticket booking platform solving the double-booking race condition via pessimistic locking, validated under 500+ concurrent transactions with zero data inconsistencies.
bullets: Solved the 'Double Booking' race condition by implementing a Pessimistic Locking strategy using a dedicated reservation table, ensuring strict ACID compliance during concurrent seat requests.
Designed a normalized database schema (3NF) with strategic indexing on search columns, optimizing query performance for high-frequency seat availability checks.
Simulated high-load scenarios using Locust, validating the locking mechanism under 500+ concurrent transactions with zero data inconsistencies.
Implemented a secure authentication flow with JWT Access/Refresh tokens and role-based access control.

projects.name: TCP Chat Application
projects.description: Developed a low-latency 1-to-1 real-time chat system using raw TCP sockets, bypassing HTTP overhead for persistent bidirectional communication with PostgreSQL-backed message persistence.
bullets: Reduced message delivery latency compared to standard HTTP polling by utilizing raw TCP sockets for persistent full-duplex communication.
Implemented persistent message storage in PostgreSQL with an indexed schema enabling efficient chat history retrieval by user and timestamp.
Engineered a connection manager that handles 200+ concurrent socket connections gracefully.
